comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout. Dream subagent session wait helpers in extensions/tau/src/dream/subagent.ts convert callback Effects into detached Promises via Effect.runPromise: waitForSessionEnd() at lines 211-263, createTurnLimitGuard().promise at 285-306, and promptFailurePromise at 437-450. The main flow then races those Promises inside Effect.tryPromise(Promise.race(...)) at 453-483. Because the callback subscriptions are started by Effect.runPromise before entering the outer Effect.tryPromise, their cleanup finalizers are attached to those inner runtimes, not to interruption of the outer dream run fiber. If the outer Effect is interrupted while awaiting the Promise race, the plain Promises continue independently until they settle, and their session subscriptions/guards are not structurally cancelled by that interruption. The current ensuring block only disposes turnLimitGuard and unsubscribeActivity; it does not cancel the detached waitForSessionEnd/promptFailure listeners. This is a real structured-concurrency bug.
